titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry Arthur Wynne to Henry F. Brennan, 9 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry F. Brennan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter is from Henry Arthur Wynne (1867-1945) to Henry F. Brennan, crown solicitor for Kings County. Henry Arthur Wynne was appointed to the vacant position of chief crown solicitor following the death of Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916).
In his letter, Wynne states that the Attorney General consents to his request for leave. Brennan's original letter requesting leave, dated 5 August, is enclosed.
This letter states that it is Brennan's intention to take leave of absence from 14 August to 5 September. He intends to stay at the Grand Hotel, Malahide, County Dublin for much of that time but will remain in contact with his Tullamore and Dublin offices. He states that he will be able to attend at the Tullamore office for some days during the period he wishes to be on leave. His business partner, Sydney Matthews, will provide cover. There are no contentious cases scheduled for the period.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5620/13588

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
9th August 1916 Leave of Absence Dear Sir,
The Attorney General consents to your having the leave
asked for. Yours truly,
Henry Arthur Wynne Henry F.Brennan Esq.,
Crown Solicitor,
Tullamore.
Telephone No.5 Tullamore
Hoey & Denning
Solicitors
George J.Hoey
Henry F.Brenan. L.L.D.
And Also At
12, Trinity St. Dublin.
Tullamore
5th August, 1916
Sir, I beg to enclose my application for leave of Absence from
the 14th inst. to the 5th prox. During this period mentioned it is my intention to stay at
the Grand Hotel, Malahide, and I will be in daily telephonic communicat-
ion with my Tullamore and Dublin Offices. It will probably be necessary for me to be in Tullamore
on some days during the period mentioned. In the event of the Crown
Solicitor's attendance being required either I or my proposed substitute who is my partner in the Dublin office
can reach Tullamore at the shortest possible notice. At the present time there are two cases returned for trial
for the Quarter Sessions commencing on the 17th October. The cases in-
volve charges of assault and malicious wounding and they do not appear
to present any unusual difficulty. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant,
Henry F. Brennan.
questionable reading
CHIEF SECRETARY'S
13588 - - 7.AUG.1916
OFFICE
I Henry F Brennan Crown Solicitor
for Kings County
beg to apply for leave of Absence from 14th August
to 5th September 1916, (inclusive).
First Schedule.
Proposed Substitute. Qualifiction. Date admitteed.
Sydney Matthews Solicitor 1915
12, Trinity St Dublin &
1, Leinster Terrace, Kingstown.
of Holy & Solicitors.
Second Schedule.
Crown Cases pending of which the Crown Solicitor has
been ordered to take charge of which may possibly
come on during his absence:-
Title of cause. Nature of Case
None
Dated this 5th day of August 1916.
Signed Henry F. Brennan. Attorny General
Do you approve? 7.VIII.16 I approve
9.8.16 Registrar
